title: The One With The Chick And The Duck

jokes.text: Some idiot customer put a suggestion in the suggestion box.
jokes.setup: Character complains about someone's stupid suggestion for roller skating waitresses
jokes.punchline: The reveal that Rachel was the 'idiot customer'
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection setup with efficient payoff - calling someone an idiot right before they're revealed

jokes.text: Rachel crashes on roller skates while carrying cocoa
jokes.setup: Monica makes Rachel cocoa while she's on roller skates
jokes.punchline: Rachel crashes dramatically, spilling everything
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy - predictable but well-executed visual gag

jokes.text: You know, with that goatee, you kind of look like Satan.
jokes.setup: Chandler has grown a goatee
jokes.punchline: Monica compares his new look to Satan
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with Monica's characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: So that's why the priest threw holy water on me.
jokes.setup: Monica says he looks like Satan
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals this has actually happened to him
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and Chandler's perfect deadpan delivery style - takes the Satan joke to absurd logical conclusion

jokes.text: I was crying because nobody believed Quincy's theory.
jokes.setup: Chandler assumes Joey was crying about Kate
jokes.punchline: Joey was actually crying about a TV medical drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - his emotional investment in TV shows over real relationships

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Who's gonna watch that?
jokes.setup: Ross excitedly announces he'll be on Discovery Channel about fossils
jokes.punchline: Rachel's immediate deflating response about the niche audience
jokes.explanation: Sharp deflation of Ross's excitement with Rachel's characteristic lack of filter

jokes.text: I saw a girl with that vest.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if he's ready to go
jokes.punchline: Chandler critiques Ross's fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler sarcasm - economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You're sturdier than Chandler. He crumbled like a piece of paper.
jokes.setup: Pete tries on roller skates
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals Chandler was terrible at skating with vivid imagery
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor for Chandler's physical ineptitude - fits character perfectly

jokes.text: Whichever's closest.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if he wants regular or decaf coffee
jokes.punchline: Pete's casual response showing his wealth/different priorities
jokes.explanation: Subtle character humor showing Pete's casual attitude toward normal decisions

jokes.text: I bought a restaurant and I'd like you to be the chef.
jokes.setup: Pete casually asks what he did today
jokes.punchline: Reveals he bought an entire restaurant as a casual gesture
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation humor - perfectly captures Pete's extreme wealth in an absurd but believable way

jokes.text: What a jerk. You want me to kick his ass?
jokes.setup: Monica says Pete offered her a restaurant
jokes.punchline: Joey completely misunderstands this as an insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey misunderstanding - his protective instincts override his comprehension

jokes.text: But, Mon, that was totally different. He was your health teacher.
jokes.setup: Monica compares accepting Pete's restaurant to not accepting a necklace in 7th grade
jokes.punchline: Rachel reveals the 7th grader was actually Monica's teacher
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - completely reframes Monica's 'innocent' childhood story into something inappropriate

jokes.text: Got a job on a riverboat?
jokes.setup: Ross enters wearing an unusual suit
jokes.punchline: Chandler's specific riverboat reference
jokes.explanation: Good specific reference that paints a clear visual picture of the suit's style

jokes.text: I like it even more on you than I did on Colonel Sanders.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if she likes his white suit
jokes.punchline: Rachel compares it to the KFC mascot
jokes.explanation: Strong visual reference that's both specific and devastating - good quotable insult

jokes.text: What were you gonna tell us? Was it how you invented the cotton gin?
jokes.setup: Ross's old-fashioned white suit
jokes.punchline: Rachel continues the historical theme with cotton gin invention
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that extends the historical mockery, though less punchy than the Colonel Sanders line

jokes.text: So I guess that's about two weeks before the topless thing kicks in.
jokes.setup: Learning the suggestion box takes 3 months to implement ideas
jokes.punchline: Rachel reveals she suggested topless waitresses
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals Rachel's suggestions are getting progressively more outrageous

jokes.text: You guys? Do you know anything about chicks? Fowl? No. Women? No.
jokes.setup: Joey asks about chicks while holding a baby chicken
jokes.punchline: Chandler clarifies both meanings and admits incompetence at both
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect economy - acknowledges the pun while using it for self-deprecating humor

jokes.text: Easy, Lenny.
jokes.setup: Joey is being overly enthusiastic about caring for the chick
jokes.punchline: Chandler references 'Of Mice and Men' character who loves things too much
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference that perfectly captures Joey's overzealous pet care - very economical

jokes.text: I got tired of waiting. That's great. I'm sorry, but I'm so happy for you.
jokes.setup: Pete says he found someone else
jokes.punchline: Monica's overly enthusiastic reaction to being rejected
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's relief and awkward over-compensation

jokes.text: I'm okay! I'm all right!
jokes.setup: Monica tries to skate into her boss's office to quit
jokes.punchline: She crashes but calls out reassuringly
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with Monica's characteristic need to appear competent even while failing

jokes.text: That's exciting. You went to Japan, made up a woman.
jokes.setup: Pete claims he met someone in Japan
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately sees through the lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her directness and intuition cutting through Pete's deception

jokes.text: No, I'm fairly intuitive. And psychic. It's a substantial gift.
jokes.setup: Pete says she's good at reading people
jokes.punchline: Phoebe escalates to claiming psychic powers
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe escalation from reasonable to absurd, delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Thanks a lot. No, I'm serious. I'm intuitive, but my memory sucks.
jokes.setup: Pete asks her to keep his secret
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately forgets what the secret was
jokes.explanation: Great ironic payoff to her psychic claims - shows her selective memory in perfect Phoebe fashion

jokes.text: I can't tell. Whatever it was went back in too quickly.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if he thinks the chick is a she
jokes.punchline: Chandler's uncomfortable description of trying to determine the chick's gender
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor about an awkward situation - fits Chandler's discomfort with the pet

jokes.text: Well, I don't think so, mister!
jokes.setup: Joey and Chandler arguing about chick care like a married couple
jokes.punchline: Joey's indignant maternal response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the domestic argument metaphor with Joey taking the maternal role

jokes.text: I don't know, maybe we weren't ready to have a chick.
jokes.setup: Joey and Chandler have been fighting since getting the pet
jokes.punchline: Chandler frames it like relationship counseling about having children
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor treating pet ownership like relationship counseling about parenthood

jokes.text: Really?
jokes.setup: Phoebe says his butt looks great in the blue suit
jokes.punchline: Ross's pleased reaction to the compliment about his butt
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Ross's vanity and need for validation

jokes.text: That aspirin dance really works.
jokes.setup: Rachel has been injured and taking aspirin
jokes.punchline: Ross observes her pain relief 'dance' with medical authority
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about pain relief behavior - nothing special but functional

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Rachel. It's nice to meet you.
jokes.setup: Rachel insists she can go to her boss's dinner party injured
jokes.punchline: Ross mimics how she'll awkwardly introduce herself while in pain
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy visualization showing how awkward she'll be while injured

jokes.text: Do you guys know how to get a chick out of a VCR?
jokes.setup: Joey asks for help getting ready
jokes.punchline: Reveals the chick has gotten stuck in electronic equipment
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - completely unexpected problem that's both funny and believable for Joey's situation

jokes.text: Since when do you think I don't wear enough of this?
jokes.setup: Ross says she should wear more makeup
jokes.punchline: Rachel questions his unsolicited beauty advice
jokes.explanation: Realistic relationship tension about unsolicited advice - more character development than comedy

jokes.text: Sophisticated like a hooker?
jokes.setup: Ross says the makeup will make her look more sophisticated
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic interpretation of his heavy-handed application
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with good economy - nice escalation of the makeup criticism

jokes.text: Does it have to do with Chandler and that sock he keeps by his bed?
jokes.setup: Monica tries to guess Phoebe's secret
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Chandler's masturbation habits
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversive humor - casual mention of adult topic with perfect economy and Monica's blunt delivery

jokes.text: No, but let's come back to that later.
jokes.setup: Monica's casual mention of Chandler's sock
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's intrigued response wanting more details
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the subversive joke with Phoebe's characteristic curiosity

jokes.text: Sure. I'll just sit next to the transsexual from purchasing.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if the makeup job is good enough for her party
jokes.punchline: Rachel's casual reference to her coworker's gender identity
jokes.explanation: Dated humor that relies on gender identity as punchline - doesn't age well and feels cheap

jokes.text: I sucked that mini-marshmallow out of your bellybutton.
jokes.setup: Ross reminisces about intimate moments to argue he should see her naked
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and awkward sexual memory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of intimate memories with absurd specificity - creates appropriate cringe

jokes.text: All I have to do is close my eyes. See?
jokes.setup: Ross claims he can see Rachel naked anytime
jokes.punchline: Ross demonstrates by closing his eyes and remembering
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment showing Ross's inappropriate boundary crossing in classic style

jokes.text: Now there are 100 of you, and I'm the king!
jokes.setup: Ross continues his inappropriate visualization
jokes.punchline: Ross's fantasy escalates to absurd proportions
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that takes the inappropriate memory into full absurdist territory

jokes.text: I feel like I'm talking to Lassie.
jokes.setup: Phoebe keeps giving yes/no responses to Monica's secret guessing
jokes.punchline: Monica compares it to communicating with the famous TV dog
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that perfectly captures the communication frustration

jokes.text: That's Yasmine Bleeth. She's a completely different kind of chick.
jokes.setup: Joey is looking at Baywatch on TV while holding the baby chick
jokes.punchline: Joey acknowledges the double meaning while appreciating both
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the chick wordplay with Joey's characteristic appreciation for attractive women

jokes.text: I love you both. But in very different ways.
jokes.setup: Joey talking to both the chick and Yasmine Bleeth on TV
jokes.punchline: Joey clarifies his different types of love for each
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his innocent sincerity about loving both a pet and attractive TV star

jokes.text: If they can't find a home for her, they kill her! I won't let that happen to little Jasmine.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks why Joey didn't return the chick
jokes.punchline: Joey reveals the dramatic stakes and names the chick after Yasmine Bleeth
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Joey's protective instincts and callback to his TV crush

jokes.text: Funny story!
jokes.setup: Joey announces he has a funny story
jokes.punchline: Camera reveals Joey now has a duck as well
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual reveal with perfect economy - Joey's understatement about acquiring another pet

jokes.text: Shut up for a second and let me just see something.
jokes.setup: Pete is apologizing for the situation
jokes.punchline: Monica interrupts to test if she's attracted to him by kissing
jokes.explanation: Good Monica moment showing her practical, experimental approach to relationships

jokes.text: Rach, you can't look fat in an x-ray.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if she'll tell her the truth about something
jokes.punchline: Ross's logical response about x-ray appearance
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with Ross's logical approach to Rachel's vanity concerns

jokes.text: That's a bad duck!
jokes.setup: The duck has apparently caused some trouble
jokes.punchline: Chandler scolds the duck like a misbehaving child
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment with Chandler adapting to reluctant pet parenthood

jokes.text: I was kind of supposed to be on TV tonight for the Discovery Channel.
jokes.setup: Ross admits he missed his TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Understated revelation of missing his big moment
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Ross's sacrifice, though delivered with appropriate understatement

jokes.text: Chicks don't swim.
jokes.setup: Joey suggests testing if the chick can swim
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact response about bird swimming abilities
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor with Chandler's logical response to Joey's experimentation

jokes.text: Well, give him a minute.
jokes.setup: They put the chick in water and it doesn't swim
jokes.punchline: Joey's optimistic belief the chick might learn
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment showing his naive optimism about animal abilities
